plan: 1. Add `drawThree` pure function to `src/domain/drawRandom.ts` — calls `drawOne` three times sequentially, returns `{ cards: [Card, Card, Card] | null; pool: DrawPool }`. Returns null if fewer than 3 cards remain. This keeps draw logic testable and outside React.

3. Create `src/features/play/DrawThreeKeepOne.tsx` component with states: 'ready' (initial, show Draw Three button), 'choosing' (three cards displayed, user picks one), 'kept' (chosen card displayed with New Round button). Uses `useRef<DrawPool>` for pool state (same pattern as SingleDraw). Shows remaining card count. Reuses `CardVisual` for card rendering. Each card in choosing phase gets a 'Keep This Card' button.

4. Create `src/features/play/DrawThreeKeepOne.module.css` — card grid (3-column on desktop, stacked on mobile), selection hover/focus states, kept-card highlight, dismiss animation (subtle opacity transition). Reuse existing CSS custom properties for consistency.
6. Update `src/App.tsx` — add route `#/play/draw-three` rendering `<DrawThreeKeepOne cards={cardDeck} />`. Keep `#/play` pointing to SingleDraw.

7. Update `src/ui/Nav/Nav.tsx` — either add a direct nav link for 'Draw Three' or change 'Play' to route to a play mode selector page. Simplest: add `{ label: 'Draw 3', href: '#/play/draw-three' }` to navItems.

8. Create `tests/unit/draw-three.test.ts` — unit tests for `drawThree` domain function: returns 3 unique cards, returns null when <3 available, does not mutate pool, all 3 cards come from the pool's available list, pool.available shrinks by 3.

9. Create `tests/unit/draw-three-keep-one.test.tsx` — component tests: renders Draw Three button initially, shows 3 cards after draw, each card has a keep button, clicking keep shows only the chosen card, unchosen cards are dismissed, New Round button appears and works, Reset restores full deck, disabled state when <3 cards remain.
10. Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to verify all checks pass.
11. Run `npm run test:e2e` if Playwright tests exist for smoke testing the new route.
testing_recommendations: Unit test `drawThree`: returns exactly 3 unique cards, returns null when pool has <3 cards, does not mutate original pool, reduces available count by 3
Unit test `drawThree` duplicate prevention: all 3 returned cards have distinct cardNumbers
Component test: initial state renders 'Draw Three' button and no cards
Component test: after drawing, exactly 3 CardVisual elements render with keep buttons
Component test: clicking a keep button dismisses the other 2 cards and shows only the kept card
Component test: kept card state shows 'New Round' button, clicking it returns to choosing state with 3 new cards
Component test: 'Reset' button restores full deck count and returns to ready state
Component test: draw button disabled when fewer than 3 cards remain in pool
Component test: keyboard accessibility — keep buttons are focusable and activatable via Enter/Space
E2E smoke test: navigate to #/play/draw-three, draw cards, keep one, verify UI state transitions
risks: Nav update: adding a separate 'Draw 3' nav item may clutter navigation — may need a play mode selector sub-page instead. Start with direct link and iterate.

Pool exhaustion edge case: if fewer than 3 cards remain after a keep, the next draw must be disabled with a clear message. Need to handle the boundary (e.g., exactly 3 remaining works, 2 remaining does not).
CSS grid for 3 cards must be responsive — 3-column layout can break on narrow mobile screens. Use media queries to stack vertically below ~640px.
The existing `drawOne` uses `Math.random()` which is not seedable — tests for `drawThree` must test structural properties (uniqueness, count, pool mutation) rather than specific card values.

Route naming: `#/play/draw-three` introduces a nested route pattern not yet used. The simple hash router in App.tsx handles exact matches, so this should work, but verify no prefix-matching issues with `#/play`.
